name: Peñascosa — circular por la Sierra de Alcaraz
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñascosa offers diverse terrain within Spain's Sierra de Alcaraz, characterized by mountainous landscapes, limestone peaks, and dense forests of pine and oak. The region features a blend of challenging ascents, technical descents, and scenic forest tracks, with elevations reaching nearly 1,800 meters. Riders can explore geological formations, ravines, and river courses, including the unique Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz. Best mountain bike trails around Peñascosa The most popular mountain bike trail is Alcaraz, Peñascosa, and Vía Verde Loop, a 28.5 miles (46.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route combines historical towns with sections of the Vía Verde, offering varied surfaces. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alcaraz and Peñascosa Loop via La Molata y Los Batanes, a moderate 28.5 miles (46.0 km) path. This trail passes through natural monuments and varied landscapes, including the Microrreserva La Molata y Los Batanes. Local mountain bikers also love the Peñascosa — circular por la Sierra de Alcaraz, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alcaraz, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñascosa is defined by limestone peaks, dense pine forests, and the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest tracks to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Peñascosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 13 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñascosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Peñascosa?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Peñascosa, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 1 easy, 25 moderate, and 17 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking in Peñascosa?
A: Mountain biking in Peñascosa offers a diverse range of difficulties. You'll find everything from moderate forest tracks to challenging mountain ascents and technical descents. The region's geomor-phology, with its crags and ravines, means some segments can be unpaved and difficult, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz is a prominent feature in the region. You can experience it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040841908/alcaraz-penascosa-and-via-verde-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz, Peñascosa, and Vía Verde Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines historical towns with sections of this unique greenway. The Vía Verde itself is built on an old railway line, offering varied surfaces from compacted earth to tarmac.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Peñascosa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1216699399/penascosa-circular-por-la-sierra-de-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñascosa — circular por la Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the stunning Sierra de Alcaraz.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 10-meter cascade on the Bogarra River. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Arquillo&lt;/a&gt;, a natural lagoon complex, and unique singular trees like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5227975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Gordo del Toril&lt;/a&gt;, are points of interest you might pass or find nearby.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore while mountain biking near Peñascosa?
A: The historical town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, a short distance away, is a significant attraction. It boasts a monumental &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, declared a National Monument, and the ruins of a 12th-century castle. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1216651984/alcaraz-and-penascosa-loop-via-la-molata-y-los-batanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz and Peñascosa Loop via La Molata y Los Batanes&lt;/a&gt; can take you through areas with such historical context.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes with significant ascents?
A: The Sierra de Alcaraz, where Peñascosa is located, offers spectacular ascents to peaks like Pico de La Sarga and Pico Almenara, which reach nearly 1,800 meters. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz – Cascada de los Batanes loop from Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1200 meters of elevation gain, providing demanding climbs and rewarding panoramic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Peñascosa?
A: The mountain biking routes in Peñascosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 13 reviews. Over 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from limestone peaks to dense pine forests, and the unique experience of the Vía Verde.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Peñascosa?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter might bring colder conditions, particularly at higher altitudes, though some trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through tunnels or over viaducts?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, which is integrated into several mountain bike routes, features tunnels and viaducts as it follows the path of an old, unfinished railway line. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnels – Alcaraz loop from Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that incorporates these unique features.

Q: Are there any mountain refuges or places for resting along the trails?
A: The municipality of Peñascosa provides mountain refuges such as Mal Paso, Cañada de Bogarra, and Fuente La Peña. These can serve as convenient resting points or bases for activities, and some may even allow for free camping, offering a chance to immerse yourself further in the natural environment.
tips: Peñascosa is a small town in the foothills of the Sierra de Alcaraz. With many trails nearby, it's a great place to start your adventure.
Nice gravel path in the Sierra de Alcaraz park along the dry river Portelano in the. The Fuente de la Peña Refuge is nearby in case you need to take shelter.
By a gravel road you reach the highest point of the area of Las Coronillas at 1450 masl you can have a panoramic view of Paterna del Madera and Peñascosa
